title: Root test script skips the pg package that the release build now includes

evidence.quote: "build": "pnpm --filter sqlfu build && pnpm --filter @sqlfu/pg build && pnpm --filter @sqlfu/ui build && pnpm --filter sqlfu-website build"

reasoning: The root build script treats @sqlfu/pg as part of the release build, but the root test script only runs sqlfu and @sqlfu/ui. The included pg tests exercise real schema diffing/materialization and migration-lock behavior for the package being built, so `pnpm test` can pass while the release-included pg package has failing behavior tests.
reproduction: Run `pnpm test` from the workspace root and observe that it expands to `pnpm --filter sqlfu test && pnpm --filter @sqlfu/ui test`, with no `pnpm --filter @sqlfu/pg test`.
recommendation: Add `pnpm --filter @sqlfu/pg test` to the root `test` script, or introduce an explicit release/CI test script that covers every package included by the root build script.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The pg tests exist, but the root command declared for this feature does not invoke them.
suggestedRegressionTest: A lightweight script assertion could verify that packages included in the root `build` script are also represented in the root release test command, or CI can simply run the corrected root `pnpm test`.
minimumFixScope: Update the root `package.json` test script to include @sqlfu/pg tests, accounting for the Postgres fixture requirement in CI.

history.reasoning: The original package.json evidence still exists at lines 26-28, but the root test script now includes `pnpm --filter @sqlfu/pg test` between the sqlfu and UI test runs. The root build script still includes `@sqlfu/pg`, so the release-built pg package is now represented by the root test command. The cited pg behavior tests also still exist: `packages/pg/test/diff-schema.test.ts` still imports `pgDialect` at line 5 and defines diffSchema tests, and `packages/pg/test/migration-lock.test.ts` still contains the real concurrency migration-lock test at lines 1-8. `packages/pg/package.json` confirms `@sqlfu/pg test` runs `vitest`. This directly addresses the reported gap; the finding is fixed.
